data.name: FrameRangeTipText
data.value: If using consecutive frames, please use the+button to obtain coordinates before entering frames

data.name: SaveAfterFolderText
data.value: Automatically open the folder after saving

data.name: HotkeyConflictMsg
data.value: This button will conflict with the MMD menu, please replace it with another button.

data.name: EnsurePathPrefixType
data.value: Make sure you have selected the path, filename prefix and image format!

data.name: PathEmpty
data.value: The export path is empty, please select the export path first!

data.name: LogSaveFail
data.value: The file was not saved successfully, maybe the input failed or the path is wrong.

data.name: LogNoFrame
data.value: Failed to get current frame number, frame number will be ignored and saved.

data.name: PromptContentText
data.value:
MMD FrameShot Save by:SaraKale
This is a small tool for quickly saving images in MMD, saving the tedious process of manually saving images.
Operating system requirements: Windows 7 SP1 and higher system versions
Main features: 1. realistic simulation button saves images and can be configured with shortcut button binding. 2. Customizable file names, image file types, and export paths, as well as additional image compression processing. 3. Multiple images can be saved within a continuous frame range. 4. Program multi language interface switching.
1. Simply run the MMD FrameShot Save.exe program. Please note that only MikuMikuDance versions 9.26 and above are supported, and lower versions are not supported. MikuMikuDance distinguishes between 32-bit and 64 bit versions, please choose the corresponding version. X32/MMD FrameShot Save_x32.exe corresponds to MikuMikuDance x86/x32 bit program X64/MMD FrameShot Save_x64.exe corresponds to the MikuMikuDance x64bit program How Do I know which platform my MMD is on? Right-click the task manager in the system taskbar, click“Details”, right-click“Select column” in the column heading, select“Platform” and the platform option will appear, go down to the Mikumikudance process and you'll see which platform it corresponds to.
2. The default language is English. You can switch to your favorite language by selecting Language in the upper right corner. There is a "↑" top button on the right side. When the program is first launched, it defaults to automatic top setting, with a constant blue light. You can manually click the button to cancel the top setting.
3. At this point, the current resolution and frame rate of the MikuMikuDance window will be read. If there are no values, it doesn't matter, it's just for reference.Exporting images will not include frame numbers.
4. Please first set the file name prefix, file type, and export path in the "Settings" tab. After setting up, a config. ini configuration file will be generated in the program directory, which will automatically record the settings. The next time you open the program, the saved settings will be read. The file naming format is as follows: [File prefix] _ [frame rate] _ [date time] _ [serial number] [Extension] Example: Camera_frame01_20250101_001.png The file types that MMD supports for exporting are: bmp、jpg、png、dds、dib、pfm、hdr
5. The save waiting time is the delay after each save, and it is recommended to set it to 5000ms or more, as MMD saves images slowly and can be adjusted moderately according to the running speed of your machine. The time conversion is as follows: 1000ms = 1s 5000ms = 5s 60000ms = 60s In addition, the timeline of MMD can be set to a maximum of 300000 frames per second (approximately 1 hour and 40 minutes, calculated at 30FPS).
6. Shortcut keys You can customize shortcut keys, only supporting Ctrl/Alt/Shift+letter/function keys, etc. Be careful not to conflict with existing shortcut keys in the MMD menu bar. If there is a conflict, you can replace other keys. This is the existing button for MMD: Alt+F、Alt+D、Alt+V、Alt+B、Alt+M、Alt+P、Alt+K、Alt+H
7. Automatically open folder after saving: After successful saving, the exported folder will be automatically opened.
8. Only single frame saving Checking this option will disable the continuous frame function and only be used for saving a single image.
9. Continuous frames This is a frame setting that allows you to set the range from the start frame to the end frame. If you want to use consecutive frames, please click the "+" button first, move the cursor to the "Frame Operation" box for positioning, and then enter the number of frames that need to be continuously captured. Because MMD cannot operate in the background memory and can only accept real physical key inputs, it will display the coordinate values of X and Y after being set.
10. Save button After setting it up, you can save the picture now! At this point, the system input method will automatically switch to the English input method. If there is no switch, please switch the input method to the English keyboard state by yourself. If it is a Windows 10 system, please press Alt+Shift/Win+Space to switch to "UK Keyboard/US Keyboard", ensuring that the input method is an English keyboard, so that there will be no interference or accidental touch when saving. After pressing the save button, the program will automatically operate. During this period, please be patient and wait for the image to be saved. There will be a log output below.
11. Pause button This is a feature used when using consecutive frames, which can forcibly pause the task midway.
12. Image compression This is the setting for compressing images simultaneously, as the original images are relatively large. If necessary, image compression can be enabled. Basically, it's easy to understand just by looking at the name. The compression quality of JPG/Webp/Avif is usually in the range of 1-100, and the smaller the value, the smaller the compression. It is generally recommended to set it in the range of 75-95. The compression level of PNG is also similar, with a maximum compression level of 9, and the smaller the value, the smaller the compression. Turn on 'Image Size' to adjust the image size, measured in px pixels, with values of width and height.Example: 1920x1080.

data.name: SwitchEnglishKeyboardFail
data.value: Cannot switch to English input method automatically, please switch manually.

data.name: EnglishInputMethodText
data.value: The software needs to be used with English input method.

data.name: PromptGetLocation
data.value: Please get the screen position at the "+" before saving!

data.name: PromptInputFrameRange
data.value: Please enter the frame rate range before saving!

data.name: invalidkey
data.value: The shortcut key setting is invalid. Please re-enter.

data.name: Registrationfail
data.value: Hotkey registration failed and may have been taken by another program.
